meta.t001: Actual Budget — Curso completo para principiantes
hero.t004: Un curso estructurado y apto para principiantes para entender los dos flujos de presupuesto de Actual y elegir el que mejor se adapte a tus necesidades.
hero.t009: Based on the official Actual docs so the tutorial stays close to the original source. Edit this tutorial on GitHub
lesson01.t004: Actual Budget is a privada, de código abierto, local-first finance app. Esta lección explains what it is, why it's worth your time, and exactly how this course is structured so you always know where you are.
lesson01.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson01.t006: Conoce what Actual Budget is and what problem it solves
lesson01.t007: Entiende the idea of an Actual presupuesto file
lesson01.t008: Conoce the difference between the Desktop Client and a Self-Hosted / Cloud Server
lesson01.t009: Have a clear map for the rest of the course
lesson01.t011: Qué hace que Actual Budget diferente?
lesson01.t012: Most presupuestación apps are cloud services - your bank details and transacción history live on a company's servers. Actual is diferente because it is local-first, de código abierto, and does not require a mandatory hosted service. Puedes keep your presupuesto on your own device, or run a servidor you control when you need sincronización and multi-device access.
lesson01.t014: When you first open Actual, you create a "presupuesto file." Everything - your accounts, transacciones, categorías, and reports - lives in that file. It can stay local on your device, or sincronización across devices when you use a servidor. If you ever want to stop using Actual, you export your data and you own it completely.
lesson01.t016: Instalación: elige uno de dos caminos
lesson01.t017: Actual has two parts: the client, which is the app you use, and the servidor, which adds sincronización, Bank Sync, navegador access, and API access. The Desktop Client is the easiest way to start; a self-hosted or cloud servidor is better when you want Actual disponible across devices.
lesson01.t019: Easiest starting point for most beginners: download the desktop app for Windows, Linux, or Mac, create a presupuesto, and start without Docker, hosting, or cloud setup.
lesson01.t021: Good for learning Actual and getting started quickly
lesson01.t022: Works offline and supports presupuesto file export/import
lesson01.t023: Can optionally run a local servidor to enable Bank Sync.
lesson01.t024: Limit: No app access remotely or from your phone
lesson01.t026: Best long-term setup for reliable remote web access from your phone, navegador, or anywhere. Puedes self-host it, or use a hosted option such as PikaPods.
lesson01.t029: Access Actual from navegador or phone navegador/PWA
lesson01.t030: Usa Bank Sync where supported and configured
lesson01.t031: Usa the Actual API and integrations
lesson01.t032: Connect the desktop app to your servidor
lesson01.t034: Start with the desktop app if you want the easiest setup. Learn Actual locally first, then move your presupuesto later to a self-hosted or cloud servidor by exporting and importing your presupuesto file. Elige a servidor when you need reliable access from multiple devices, Bank Sync where supported, or API access. If you do not want to use the command line, PikaPods is the simplest hosted option.
lesson01.t036: What does a presupuestación app actually do for you?
lesson01.t037: A presupuestación app does not magically fix everything, but it gives you clarity. It helps you answer two important questions: where did my dinero go, and can I afford this with confidence?
lesson01.t038: In Actual, you do this through accounts, transacciones, categorías, and your mensual presupuesto. Do not worry if those words are new; each one has its own lesson in this course.
lesson01.t040: You add your accounts, record or import transacciones, then assign each transacción to a categoría like Groceries or Rent. After that, the presupuesto page and reports start showing the real picture of your gasto.
lesson01.t043: This is a short 9-lesson course. The goal is not to memorize everything; it is to build your presupuesto step by step without big jumps.
lesson01.t044: Foundation — What Actual is, the two presupuesto modes, and which workflow fits you.
lesson01.t045: Core Setup — Build your presupuesto, add accounts, and understand credit cards.
lesson01.t046: Daily Usa — Handle transacciones and use rules to reduce manual work.
lesson01.t047: Insights & Improvement — Read reports, then use tips and FAQ to keep improving.
lesson01.t049: Read lecciones 01 and 02 first to understand the idea. Then go to lesson 03 and start building your actual presupuesto. Do not try to configure every feature at the beginning; you will learn what you need at the right time.
lesson01.t052: You do not need to change anything right now. Just know that Settings is where you will return later to change presupuestación mode, date and number formats, and other preferences.
lesson01.t053: 📍Settings — access it from the arrow next to the presupuesto name or from More → Settings in the sidebar.
lesson01.t056: Starting Fresh — setting up a new file, accounts, and first categorías
lesson01.t057: Installing Actual — ways to run Actual with or without a servidor
lesson01.t058: Download Desktop App — download the desktop app from GitHub
lesson01.t059: PikaPods setup guide — run Actual with a simple hosted servidor
lesson01.t060: Settings — general preferences and presupuestación method
lesson01.t062: Two Budgeting Modes: Which One to Start With
lesson02.t004: Actual offers two valid presupuestación workflows. Envelope is Actual's predeterminado and recomendado efectivo-allocation method, while Tracking is a lighter traditional forecast workflow for ingresos and expense targets. Elige the workflow that fits your situation - Lección 03 will automatically adapt.
lesson02.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson02.t006: Entiende what Tracking Budget mode is and how it works
lesson02.t007: Entiende what Envelope (zero-sum) mode is and how it works
lesson02.t008: Elige the mode that fits your need for control or awareness
lesson02.t009: Conoce how to activate your chosen mode in Settings
lesson02.t011: Actual predeterminado and recomendado efectivo-control method
lesson02.t012: Presupuesto only dinero you actually have. When ingresos arrives it becomes Available Funds, then you assign it to expense and saving categorías or hold it for next mes.
lesson02.t013: Shows whether you can afford gasto before it happens
lesson02.t014: Every dollar is assigned or intentionally held for next mes
lesson02.t015: Overspending requires funding or affects next mes's To Budget
lesson02.t019: Set mensual targets for expected ingresos and expected gastos, then compare reality against the plan. This workflow does not require you to cuenta for every disponible dollar or fund every overspend from another categoría.
lesson02.t020: Good when you already have a comfortable efectivo buffer
lesson02.t021: Shows variance from target; does not reserve disponible efectivo
lesson02.t022: Funds do not automatically roll over mes to mes
lesson02.t023: Your chosen mode — lighter planning and awareness
lesson02.t028: Available efectivo: dinero you actually have now
lesson02.t029: Forecast: expected ingresos and expected expense targets
lesson02.t031: Ingresos becomes Available Funds only when received
lesson02.t032: You presupuesto expected ingresos for the mes
lesson02.t034: You assign disponible dinero to expense and saving categorías
lesson02.t035: You set targets for what you expect to spend or save
lesson02.t037: Funding decision. Move dinero from another categoría, or let overspending reduce next mes's To Budget
lesson02.t038: Variance from target. Increasing the amount updates the forecast and lowers Projected Savings; it does not move real efectivo
lesson02.t040: Categoría balances roll forward, and Hold for Next Month can reserve dinero you do not want to use now
lesson02.t041: Funds do not automatically roll over mes to mes
lesson02.t043: People who need efectivo control and a clear answer to: can I afford this gasto?
lesson02.t044: People with a comfortable efectivo buffer who want gasto awareness and ingresos/expense targets with lower maintenance
lesson02.t045: You've chosen Envelope Budget. This is Actual's predeterminado and recomendado method for controlling disponible efectivo. Head to Lección 03 to see how it works.
lesson02.t047: How to enable Tracking Budget mode in the app
lesson02.t048: Actual starts in Envelope mode by predeterminado. If you choose Tracking as your ingresos-and-expense forecast workflow, change this setting before building the presupuesto. Puedes change modes later, but do not use switching as the main plan because historical presupuesto values do not always carry the same meaning between modes.
lesson02.t049: Settings → click "Switch to tracking presupuestación." Do this before entering presupuesto numbers so you start with the right workflow.
lesson02.t051: Click the arrow next to the presupuesto name, or go to More → Settings in the sidebar.
lesson02.t053: It's near the top of the Presupuesto section in Settings.
lesson02.t054: Done — the presupuesto view updates immediately
lesson02.t055: The columns change from "To Budget / Available" to "Budgeted / Spent / Saldo."
lesson02.t058: Envelope presupuestación — the predeterminado efectivo-control workflow
lesson02.t059: Tracking Budget — the traditional ingresos/expense forecast workflow
lesson02.t060: Settings — where to change Budgeting Method
lesson03.t004: This is where you learn how the presupuesto page works in each workflow. Usa the toggle below to compare, but choose one mode in the app based on your needs.
lesson03.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson03.t006: Entiende how categorías organize your presupuesto
lesson03.t007: Conoce how Envelope Budgeting uses To Budget
lesson03.t008: Conoce how Tracking Budgeting uses mensual targets
lesson03.t009: Entiende what overspending means in each mode
lesson03.t010: Design a simple starting categoría structure
lesson03.t014: Categorías: the buckets your dinero flows into
lesson03.t015: Every presupuesto-impacting non-transferencia transacción should be assigned to a categoría. Categorías are how Actual knows what your dinero was spent on - without them, you have a list of transacciones with less meaning. This applies to both modes.
lesson03.t016: Actual lets you group categorías - "Groceries" and "Eating Out" can both sit inside a "Food" group. This keeps the presupuesto page readable and lets you see group-level totals at a glance.
lesson03.t017: Ingresos categorías appear in the presupuesto too, but Actual keeps them in one ingresos group. Agrega your ingresos sources there, such as Salary or Freelance.
lesson03.t019: On the Presupuesto page, the left column is a list of your categoría groups, each expandable. You set a "Budgeted" amount next to each categoría. As transacciones come in and are categorized, the columns update automatically.
lesson03.t021: "To Budget": decide what to do with disponible dinero
lesson03.t022: In Envelope Budget, you do not presupuesto projected ingresos. When ingresos actually arrives, it becomes Available Funds. Then you decide: assign it to expense and saving categorías this mes, or hold part of it for next mes with Hold for Next Month.
lesson03.t024: Click the Budgeted column next to each expense or saving categoría and enter an amount. Start with essentials like rent, food, and bills. If dinero remains that you do not want to use this mes, you can hold it for next mes. The practical goal is for To Budget to reach zero because every dollar has a clear decision.
lesson03.t055: At the top of the Envelope Budget page, the summary bar shows what you actually have for this mes: Available Funds, anything overspent from last mes, what you have Budgeted for this mes, and what you chose to Hold for next month. Those numbers roll up into To Budget or Overbudget.
lesson03.t057: what you have to allocate this mes
lesson03.t059: reduces what you can allocate now
lesson03.t061: what you assigned to categorías this mes
lesson03.t063: dinero you intentionally held for next mes
lesson03.t065: Above zero = remaining To Budget | Below zero = Overbudget
lesson03.t067: To hold dinero for next mes, click the To Budget amount and choose Hold for next month. Press Enter to hold all disponible dinero, or enter a custom amount. This is optional: you can leave To Budget alone, but held dinero leaves this mes and appears in next mes's To Budget.
lesson03.t068: When a categoría goes red in Envelope mode
lesson03.t069: Overspent a categoría? Cover it now or accept the impact later
lesson03.t070: This is the heart and power of Envelope mode. When a categoría is overspent, you have two clear options: move dinero from another categoría to cover it, or let the overspending reduce next mes's To Budget. This makes the impact visible before it surprises you later.
lesson03.t077: Because you're consciously deciding: is eating out worth sacrificing entertainment? That question is what changes gasto habits over the long term.
lesson03.t079: Surpluses roll over — building a cushion over time
lesson03.t080: If you spend less than budgeted in a categoría, the surplus rolls forward automatically to next mes and adds to that categoría's saldo. Over months, categorías like "Car Maintenance" build up enough to cover big repairs without any crisis.
lesson03.t082: When dinero arrives, assign disponible funds to categorías or use Hold for Next Month until To Budget reaches zero. Weekly, import and categorize transacciones. If a categoría overspends, fund it from another categoría or accept its impact on next mes. When a new mes starts, create a new presupuesto or copy last mes's presupuesto and adjust what you learned. At mes end, review Reports.
lesson03.t084: Categorías: the buckets your dinero flows into
lesson03.t085: Every presupuesto-impacting non-transferencia transacción should be assigned to a categoría. Categorías turn a raw list of transacciones into meaningful information. Puedes group categorías (e.g. "Food", "Housing") for better organisation.
lesson03.t086: Ingresos categorías appear in the presupuesto too, but Actual keeps them in one ingresos group. Agrega your ingresos sources there, such as Salary or Freelance.
lesson03.t087: Start with 10–15 broad categorías. After one mes of real data you'll know which need splitting.
lesson03.t089: Set mensual targets: your forecast for the mes
lesson03.t090: For each ingresos categoría (like "Salary"), enter what you expect to receive. For each expense categoría, enter your gasto target. These are forecasts, not contracts. Being wrong is fine - the goal is a baseline you can refine.
lesson03.t091: How to find good starting numbers:
lesson03.t092: Open 2–3 months of bank statements and estimate your average per categoría. If unsure, presupuesto generously.
lesson03.t122: The mensual summary: your presupuesto's scoreboard
lesson03.t123: At the top of the Tracking Budget page sits a summary bar comparing your budgeted amounts (the plan) against your actual amounts (reality).
lesson03.t126: What you planned to receive vs what actually arrived
lesson03.t129: What you planned to spend vs what you actually spent
lesson03.t134: When a categoría goes red in Tracking mode
lesson03.t135: Red means variance from the target you planned, not a efectivo envelope that must be refilled. Puedes leave it as a review signal, or adjust the target if the original forecast was unrealistic.
lesson03.t139:
If you raise the target
Projected Savings drops
lesson03.t140: Changing the number in Tracking mode updates the forecast only.
lesson03.t141: Projected Savings will decrease, but no real efectivo is reserved or moved from another categoría. If you need to know whether dinero is disponible before gasto, that is Envelope mode's job.
lesson03.t144: Start of mes: copy last mes's presupuesto
lesson03.t145: Click the copy icon at the top of the presupuesto columns. Adjust anything you know will be diferente.
lesson03.t147: Set aside 10 minutes. Rules will automate most of this after a few weeks.
lesson03.t149: Decide whether the target was unrealistic and should be updated, or whether gasto needs to come down for the rest of this mes or next mes.
lesson03.t151: The Gasto report shows which categorías consistently run over. Usa this data to set better targets next mes.
lesson03.t153: Designing your categorías — a starting framework
lesson03.t154: The right categorías are personal, but here's a framework that works for most people. Start broad and refine. Fewer categorías is better to start - you can always split later.
lesson03.t156: Ingresos categorías live inside a single ingresos group.
lesson03.t161: Concrete home and utility costs that are easy to recognize.
lesson03.t167: Regular day-to-day gasto that usually happens every mes.
lesson03.t173: Recurring commitments, not just entertainment subscriptions.
lesson03.t179: Money you're deliberately setting aside for future costs or goals.
lesson03.t185: Flexible personal gasto that does not fit housing, diario living, bills, or savings.
lesson03.t190: Don't spend more than 10 minutes on categorías before you have real transacción data. Set up something reasonable now, then refine it after your first import.
lesson03.t191: Returns and reimbursements: If a store refunds a purchase, categorize the refund back to the original gasto categoría instead of treating it as new ingresos. For reimbursements from another person, check the official docs for the right workflow depending on whether you fronted the dinero.
lesson03.t194: How Budgeting Works — To Budget, overspending, and the mensual workflow
lesson03.t195: Categorías — adding categorías/groups, merging, and notes
lesson03.t196: Returns and Reimbursements — when dinero should flow back to a categoría
lesson03.t198: Cuentas: Adding and Managing Your Money
lesson04.t003: Cuentas — Adding and Managing Where Your Money Lives
lesson04.t004: Actual needs to know about the accounts you spend from, and you can also add other accounts for net worth. Esta lección focuses on where dinero lives: On Presupuesto vs Off Presupuesto accounts, common cuenta types, how to add them, and how to record transfers between your own accounts.
lesson04.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson04.t006: Conoce the difference between On Presupuesto and Off Presupuesto accounts
lesson04.t007: Conoce how to add all your accounts with the right settings
lesson04.t008: Elige the right cuenta type: checking, savings, efectivo, credit card, or tracking
lesson04.t009: Entiende transfers between your own accounts and when they need a categoría
lesson04.t011: On Presupuesto vs Off Presupuesto: choose the right cuenta role
lesson04.t012: Actual makes a distinction between accounts that affect your mensual presupuesto and accounts that are only tracked for net worth. Before adding any cuenta, ask: should gasto from this cuenta affect my presupuesto, or do I only want to track its value?
lesson04.t013: That decision determines whether the cuenta should be On Presupuesto or Off Presupuesto. Everyday gasto accounts are usually On Presupuesto, while investments, loans, and large assets are usually Off Presupuesto because they are for tracking only.
lesson04.t014: ✅ On Presupuesto — affects your mensual presupuesto
lesson04.t015: Usa this for accounts you spend from or use to pay gastos. Transacciones in these accounts are categorized and appear in your presupuesto and reports.
lesson04.t020: 📊 Off Presupuesto — tracking and net worth only
lesson04.t021: Usa this for accounts or assets you want to include in net worth, but not for day-to-day categoría gasto.
lesson04.t026: You cannot change an Off Presupuesto cuenta to On Presupuesto later. Always double-check this setting. Usa On Presupuesto for any active credit card; only consider Off Presupuesto if there will be no new purchases and the card will be closed after payoff.
lesson04.t029: When you first open Actual, a large Agrega Cuenta button is shown in the centre of the screen. Clicking it starts a simple wizard. Puedes add more accounts at any time using + Agrega cuenta at the bottom of the sidebar.
lesson04.t030: For each cuenta, enter the current saldo shown in your bank's app. Start from the beginning of the current mes, or another recent date, instead of going far back in history. That saldo becomes the opening transacción in Actual.
lesson04.t031: 📍The Agrega Cuenta button — shown prominently when you first open Actual, or disponible in the sidebar at any time.
lesson04.t032: 📍The cuenta creation dialog — choose a name, cuenta type, whether it's On or Off Presupuesto, and enter the current saldo from your bank's app.
lesson04.t033: 📍A completed cuenta form — clear name, correct type, On Presupuesto selected for a checking cuenta.
lesson04.t034: 📍Once created, accounts appear in the left sidebar. On Presupuesto accounts show at the top; Off Presupuesto at the bottom.
lesson04.t036: Transfers: moving dinero between your own accounts
lesson04.t037: When you move dinero between two accounts you own - checking to savings, or paying a credit card - use a Transferencia instead of a normal transacción. If both accounts are On Presupuesto, it does not need a categoría because the dinero stays inside your presupuesto. If one side is Off Presupuesto, the On Presupuesto side needs a categoría.
lesson04.t039: In the Beneficiario field, click Make Transferencia and choose the destination cuenta. Actual automatically creates matching entries in both accounts - a debit in one, a credit in the other. They're linked: editing one updates the other.
lesson04.t040: 📍Click "Make Transferencia" in the beneficiario dropdown to see your accounts and choose the destination.
lesson04.t041: 📍A completed transferencia — both entries are linked. Updating the note in one automatically updates the other.
lesson04.t043: When do I categorize a transferencia?
lesson04.t044: A transferencia means moving dinero between your own accounts. The categoría regla depends on the cuenta types: are both accounts On Presupuesto, or is one of them Off Presupuesto?
lesson04.t049: Money stays inside the presupuesto; only the cuenta changes.
lesson04.t051: Money leaves the presupuesto, so categorize the purpose.
lesson04.t053: Money enters the presupuesto, so categorize the source/context.
lesson04.t055: Both accounts are outside the presupuesto; no categoría is affected.
lesson04.t058: Cuentas & Transacciones overview — On/Off Presupuesto accounts and adding accounts
lesson04.t059: Transfers — transfers between accounts and transferencia categorías
lesson04.t061: Tarjetas de crédito: The Part Everyone Gets Wrong
lesson05.t003: Tarjetas de crédito: The Part Everyone Gets Wrong
lesson05.t004: More questions are asked about credit cards in Actual than any other topic. Esta lección explains the underlying concept first, then walks through a complete worked example so there are no surprises.
lesson05.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson05.t006: Entiende how Actual treats credit cards (the core concept)
lesson05.t007: Conoce what happens to your presupuesto when you swipe the card
lesson05.t008: Conoce how to record credit card payments correctly
lesson05.t009: Entiende what to do if you carry a saldo
lesson05.t011: The mental model: credit cards hold negative dollars
lesson05.t012: In Actual, a credit card is just another cuenta - but it holds negative dinero. When you create the cuenta, its saldo is negative (what you currently owe). As you make purchases, the saldo gets more negative. When you pay the card, positive dollars from your checking cuenta cancel out the negative ones.
lesson05.t013: The most important thing to understand: your presupuesto categorías are updated at the moment of purchase, not when you pay the bill. When you buy $50 of groceries on your Visa, your Groceries categoría decreases by $50 immediately. Paying the card later is just accounting - moving dinero between accounts.
lesson05.t014: Why this is better than how you might be thinking about it
lesson05.t015: Some people think of credit card gasto as "not real dinero yet" until the bill arrives. Actual disagrees - and rightly so. You've committed to that $50 the moment you bought the groceries. Recording it immediately means your presupuesto always reflects reality, not a mes-old picture.
lesson05.t017: Tracing a purchase end to end
lesson05.t018: Let's trace exactly what happens when you spend $50 on groceries with your credit card, then pay the card at mes end.
lesson05.t019: Paso 1: You buy $50 of groceries on your Visa
lesson05.t022: Your Groceries presupuesto reduced by $50 right now. The Visa cuenta records that you owe $50 more.
lesson05.t023: Paso 2: At mes end, you pay $50 to Visa from Checking
lesson05.t027: This is a Transferencia - no categoría needed. Your presupuesto was already updated at purchase time. Paying the card is just two accounts settling up.
lesson05.t029: Paying in full each mes — the full workflow
lesson05.t030: Agrega your credit card as an On Presupuesto cuenta
lesson05.t031: Usa On Presupuesto for any active card. Only consider Off Presupuesto if there will be no new purchases and the card will be closed after payoff. Enter the current saldo you owe as a negative number.
lesson05.t032: Make purchases — categorize each one when it appears
lesson05.t033: Each purchase is recorded against the relevant categoría. In Envelope mode it affects the categoría's disponible saldo; in Tracking mode it appears as actual gasto against the planned target.
lesson05.t034: When your statement arrives, reconcile the card cuenta
lesson05.t035: Click the 🔒 lock icon → enter the statement saldo → confirm each transacción. This catches anything that might be missing.
lesson05.t036: Record the payment as a Transferencia
lesson05.t037: In your Checking cuenta, add a transacción. Set Beneficiario to "Transferencia to [Card Name]" and enter the statement amount. No categoría needed.
lesson05.t038: 📍End-of-mes presupuesto — every card purchase has been categorized. No categoría is overspent, so the full statement can be paid.
lesson05.t039: 📍After the Transferencia payment — the card saldo moves back towards zero. Presupuesto categorías unchanged.
lesson05.t040: 📍The presupuesto after payment — identical to before the payment. This confirms the concept: gasto was recorded at purchase time, not at payment time.
lesson05.t041: The golden regla: Do not rely on the card saldo alone. Watch how card purchases affect your categorías: in Envelope mode, make sure the categoría is funded; in Tracking mode, monitor actual gasto against the target.
lesson05.t043: If you carry a saldo (paying minimum payments)
lesson05.t044: If you can't pay the full statement saldo one mes, you are carrying debt. This is a more detailed workflow than this lesson, but the big idea in Actual is to separate old debt from newly funded purchases.
lesson05.t045: Crea a "Tarjeta de crédito Debt" categoría group, with one debt categoría per card.
lesson05.t046: Set those debt categorías to Rollover Overspending so the negative card saldo is not counted twice.
lesson05.t047: Categorize the starting saldo, interest/fees, and any purchases you will not pay on the next statement to that card's debt categoría.
lesson05.t048: Record the actual payment as a Transferencia from Checking to the card, and always pay at least the statement minimum.
lesson05.t049: Because carrying debt has its own mensual steps and payment calculation, follow Actual's original guide: Carrying Debt.
lesson05.t052: Tarjetas de crédito and Actual Budget — the core mental model
lesson05.t053: Paying in Full — official example for paying the statement mensual
lesson05.t054: Carrying Debt — detailed workflow when you cannot pay the full statement
lesson06.t004: Actual is only as useful as the transacción data inside it. Esta lección covers how transacciones get in, what each row means, splits, payees, tags, filters, duplicates, and reconciliation so your data stays trustworthy.
lesson06.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson06.t006: Conoce the main ways to get transacciones into Actual
lesson06.t007: Entiende Bank Sync and when to use it
lesson06.t008: Be able to import a CSV file from your bank
lesson06.t009: Conoce how to handle split transacciones, duplicates, and reconciliation
lesson06.t011: The main ways to get transacciones in
lesson06.t012: Elige based on your bank's capabilities and how much automation you want. Most people end up using File Import as a semanal habit, or Bank Sync if their bank is supported.
lesson06.t014: Connect through a supported provider, then click Bank Sync when you want to fetch nuevas transacciones.
lesson06.t019: Download from your bank, import manually. 5 minutes once a week.
lesson06.t022: Type each transacción. Useful for efectivo gasto or when other options aren't disponible.
lesson06.t024: Why is OFX better than CSV? OFX files include a unique bank-assigned ID for every transacción. Actual uses this ID to automatically detect duplicates — meaning you can safely import the same time period twice without getting duplicate entries. CSV files don't have this, so duplicate detection is less reliable.
lesson06.t027: Bank Sync is optional. If your bank and regional provider are supported, you can connect the cuenta and click Bank Sync to fetch nuevas transacciones. It does not remove the need to review: you still categorize transacciones, check Rules, and reconcile accounts.
lesson06.t029: You use the desktop app with its local servidor, or an actual-servidor setup
lesson06.t030: Your bank is supported through SimpleFIN, Pluggy.ai, or GoCardless for existing users
lesson06.t031: You want a faster semanal routine than downloading files manually
lesson06.t033: Start with a recent opening saldo; avoid unnecessary old history
lesson06.t034: Bank sincronización credentials are stored on the local servidor or the servidor you use
lesson06.t035: Sync is not fully automático; you click Bank Sync when needed
lesson06.t036: For a calmer start, create the cuenta with a correct saldo on a recent date, then enable Bank Sync. Conciliación is easier when only nuevas transacciones arrive after your start point.
lesson06.t038: Importing a CSV file from your bank
lesson06.t039:
Log into your bank and download a transacción file
Look under "Transacción History," "Download Statement," or similar. Elige OFX if disponible; CSV otherwise. Select a date range from the start of this mes.
lesson06.t040: In Actual: open the cuenta → click Import → select the file
lesson06.t041:
Map CSV columns to Actual's fields
Tell Actual which column in your CSV is the Date, which is the Beneficiario name, and which is the Amount. The live preview shows how Actual will interpret each row.
lesson06.t042:
Fix date format if dates look wrong
Try diferente format options (DD/MM/YYYY vs MM/DD/YYYY). If gastos show as positive numbers, toggle "Flip amount."
lesson06.t043:
Click Import
Actual automatically avoids duplicating transacciones it has already seen.
lesson06.t044: 📍The CSV import screen — map each column to the right field. The preview shows how Actual will interpret the data before you commit.
lesson06.t046: Transacción anatomy: what each row means
lesson06.t047: Once transacciones are inside Actual, your main job is review and cleanup. You do not need to edit everything, but you should understand the key fields so you can categorize, search, and reconcile confidently.
lesson06.t051: Beneficiario — merchant, person, or transferencia cuenta
lesson06.t054: Categoría — where gasto appears in the presupuesto
lesson06.t056: Cleared — has it appeared at the bank?
lesson06.t058: Your simple routine: review nuevas transacciones, clean the beneficiario when needed, choose the categoría, then let Rules learn from your decisions.
lesson06.t060: Split Transactions: one transacción, multiple categorías
lesson06.t061: Sometimes one bank transacción contains multiple kinds of gasto. Example: one supermarket trip includes groceries, household supplies, and a gift. Instead of creating separate transacciones, split the transacción into child lines, each with its own categoría and amount.
lesson06.t062: 📍Split Transacción controls — add split lines, distribute the remainder, or unsplit when needed.
lesson06.t063: Open the transacción's Categoría and choose Split Transacción
lesson06.t064: A child row appears under the original transacción.
lesson06.t066: Give each part its own categoría and amount.
lesson06.t067: Make sure the total equals the original transacción
lesson06.t068: The child rows must add up to the parent transacción amount.
lesson06.t069: Be careful with bulk editing split transacciones. If you need to change one, open the split so you can see each child line clearly.
lesson06.t072: Three features that make managing transacciones much faster once you have real data coming in.
lesson06.t073: Payees — cleaning up ugly bank names
lesson06.t074: Banks give payees names like TARGET DEBIT CRD ACH TRAN CO ID:XXX15170. From More → Payees, you can rename them, merge duplicates, and set predeterminado categorías. Actual also learns from your renaming and creates Rules automatically - so you only clean up each beneficiario once.
lesson06.t075: 📍More → Payees: search for all variations of a beneficiario name to merge them into one clean entry.
lesson06.t076: 📍After merging — all variations become one beneficiario. Click the name to rename it to something clean like "Morrisons."
lesson06.t078: Agrega #hashtags to any transacción's Notes field to group transacciones that cross categorías. Useful for tracking the real cost of a holiday (flights in Transport, hotels in Accommodation, meals in Eating Out - all tagged #spain2025).
lesson06.t079: 📍Type # in the Notes field to add a tag — you'll see autocomplete for existing tags.
lesson06.t080: 📍The Tags management page (sidebar) — assign colours to tags and view all transacciones for any tag.
lesson06.t081: Bulk editing — update many transacciones at once
lesson06.t082: Check the box on the left of multiple transacciones, then use the toolbar to change categoría, beneficiario, notes, cuenta, or delete them all at once. Usa Filters first to narrow down to exactly what you want to change.
lesson06.t083: Bulk editing doesn't work well with split transacciones. Avoid selecting split transacciones when using bulk actions.
lesson06.t086: The Filter button above any transacción list lets you narrow what you see using multiple stacked conditions. This is far more powerful than text search - you can combine date ranges, categorías, amounts, payees, and tags.
lesson06.t087: 📍The Filter dropdown — choose from date, categoría, beneficiario, amount, cuenta, notes, and tags. Combine as many as you need.
lesson06.t088: 📍Multiple stacked filters — here filtering by a #tag + categoría + year to find the total cost of running a specific car in 2024.
lesson06.t089: 📍Save filters you use regularly — click "Unsaved filter" → "Save new filter" to give it a name.
lesson06.t092: If you enter a transacción manually and then later import the same one from your bank, you'll have a duplicate. Actual gives you a way to merge them cleanly.
lesson06.t094: Select both duplicate transacciones, then press <kbd>G</kbd> or use the dropdown → Merge. Actual keeps the more reliable version (bank-synced wins over imported, imported wins over manual) and copies any missing information (categoría, notes) from the dropped one to the kept one.
lesson06.t095: 📍Select two matching transacciones and press G — Actual merges them into one, keeping the most reliable version.
lesson06.t097: Conciliación: verifying your records against your bank
lesson06.t098: Conciliación is the habit of comparing Actual's transacción list to your official bank statement and confirming they match. It catches missing transacciones, accidental duplicates, and amounts you may have mistyped.
lesson06.t100: Click the 🔒 lock icon in the top-right of any cuenta. Enter your current bank saldo, then click Reconcile. Actual shows you a "Difference" number. Go through transacciones ticking the ones that appear on your statement (grey circle = unconfirmed, green = confirmed). When Difference reaches $0, click Lock transacciones to lock the matched transacciones.
lesson06.t101: 📍Click the green saldo in the cuenta header to see Cleared vs Uncleared totals — helps you know where the discrepancy is before you start reconciling.
lesson06.t102: You don't need to reconcile every day. Once a mes is fine for most people — just make sure you do it at least mensual if you import transacciones manually.
lesson06.t105: Importing Transacciones — Bank Sync, file import, CSV, and manual entry
lesson06.t106: Connecting Your Bank — Bank Sync and supported providers
lesson06.t107: Merging Duplicate Transacciones — duplicate merging and kept-transacción logic
lesson06.t108: Split Transactions — splitting one transacción across categorías
lesson06.t109: Payees, Tags, Bulk Actions, and Filters — transacción cleanup and management tools
lesson06.t110: Conciliación — matching accounts and locking transacciones
lesson06.t111: Transfers — important when importing both sides of a transferencia
lesson06.t113: Rules & Automation: Let Actual Do the Work For You
lesson07.t003: Rules & Automation: Let Actual Do the Work For You
lesson07.t004: Once your transacciones are coming in and getting cleaned up correctly, use Rules and Schedules to reduce repetitive work. Rules clean and categorize imported transacciones, while Schedules help you see recurring and expected transacciones before they happen.
lesson07.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson07.t006: Entiende what rules are and how they work
lesson07.t007: Conoce how Actual creates rules automatically from your behaviour
lesson07.t008: Conoce how to view, edit, and create rules manually
lesson07.t009: Entiende regla stages and how Schedules support automation
lesson07.t011: What a regla is: an "if → then" instruction
lesson07.t012: Every time a transacción is imported, Actual runs it through all your rules in order. A regla has two parts: conditions (when to fire) and actions (what to change). If all conditions match, all actions are applied.
lesson07.t013: The most common use is cleaning up ugly beneficiario names and setting categorías automatically. For example:
lesson07.t014: If all these conditions are true…
lesson07.t021: This turns AMAZON.COM*5C7QC7MH0 AM 10/26 into "Amazon / Shopping" every single time - automatically.
lesson07.t023: Actual writes most rules for you automatically
lesson07.t024: You don't need to build your regla library from scratch. Actual watches your behaviour and creates rules based on what you do:
lesson07.t025:
When you rename a beneficiario
Actual asks: "Apply this rename automatically in future?" Click yes and it creates a pre-stage regla. That ugly bank name will never appear again.
lesson07.t026:
When you categorize a transacción
After you assign the same beneficiario to a categoría a couple of times, Actual creates a regla to apply that categoría automatically going forward. After a few weeks of use, most transacciones categorize themselves on import.
lesson07.t027: The payoff: After a mes or two of regular use, importing a week's worth of transacciones takes about 2 minutes — Actual cleans up the names, assigns categorías, and you just review and confirm. This is what makes the system sustainable long-term.
lesson07.t037: Matches any item in a list
lesson07.t043: Conditions can check: imported beneficiario, beneficiario, cuenta, categoría, date, notes, amount (inflow or outflow). All string matching is case-insensitive.
lesson07.t044: Actions can set: beneficiario, categoría, notes, cuenta, date, amount, cleared status. They can also append or prepend text to notes.
lesson07.t047: Rules run in three stages. Knowing this prevents confusion when multiple rules affect the same transacción.
lesson07.t048: Pre-stage runs first, always. Actual automatically puts beneficiario-renaming rules here. This ensures beneficiario names are cleaned up before any categorization rules run.
lesson07.t049: Default-stage is where most rules live. Within this stage, more specific rules (using is) automatically rank after broader ones (using contains), so the specific regla always wins.
lesson07.t050: Post-stage runs last, always. Usa this for rules that must override everything else. Most users never need this.
lesson07.t053:
Go to More → Rules in the sidebar
See all your rules, including the ones Actual created automatically for you.
lesson07.t054:
Click "edit" on any regla to view it in detail
The editor shows a live list of existing transacciones that match your conditions — excellent feedback before saving.
lesson07.t055:
Select transacciones and click "Apply actions" to backfill
Retroactively apply a regla to past transacciones — great for re-categorizing a batch of old records in one click.
lesson07.t056: The regla editor is also a powerful bulk editor. Crea a temporary regla with conditions that match what you want to change, apply actions to selected transacciones, then delete the regla. Much faster than editing transacciones one by one.
lesson07.t059: Schedules help you see upcoming transacciones before they happen: salary, rent, subscriptions, loan payments, or credit card autopay. They can be recurring or one-time, and can be entered automatically or left for manual approval.
lesson07.t060: 📍Schedules overview — recurring or expected transacciones appear here so you can see what is coming before it happens.
lesson07.t067: Show upcoming items in the cuenta before payment date
lesson07.t068: Reduce manual entry for recurring transacciones
lesson07.t069: Can be linked with Rules for categorías and notes
lesson07.t071: Start with only three or four schedules: salary, rent, key subscriptions, and card autopay if you use it. Agrega more when you notice a recurring pattern.
lesson07.t074: Rules — conditions, fields, stages, and automático rules
lesson07.t075: Rules Examples — custom rules and Bank Sync transferencia examples
lesson07.t076: Schedules — recurring and expected transacciones
lesson07.t077: Rule Action Templating — experimental advanced regla actions
lesson07.t079: Reports: Understanding What Your Data Is Telling You
lesson08.t003: Reports: What Your Data Is Telling You
lesson08.t004: Reports turn your categorized transacciones into honest insights about your financial life. After a mes or two of real data, this becomes the most valuable part of Actual.
lesson08.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson08.t006: Conoce which reports and cards Actual provides
lesson08.t007: Conoce when to use Cash Flow, Net Worth, and Gasto Analysis
lesson08.t008: Conoce when Calendar cards and Custom Reports are useful
lesson08.t009: Entiende the experimental reports at a high level
lesson08.t011: The Reports dashboard: your financial mirror
lesson08.t012: The Reports section is separate from the presupuesto page. Where the presupuesto page tells you what you planned to spend, Reports tell you what actually happened. After even a mes of use, the reports reveal things about your gasto that will surprise you.
lesson08.t013: 📍The Reports Dashboard — multiple widgets give you a complete financial overview. The dashboard is fully customizable; you can add, remove, and resize widgets.
lesson08.t014: Small addition: besides the graphs and reports below, the Reports dashboard can also include Summary cards for a quick number and Text widgets for notes or reminders on the dashboard.
lesson08.t016: Cash Flow: "Did I save dinero this mes?"
lesson08.t017: Cash Flow only looks at On Presupuesto accounts, showing their saldo over time along with separate ingresos and expense visualizations. It gives you a picture of how ingresos and gasto affected your disponible dinero.
lesson08.t018: 📍Cash Flow — focuses on budgeted accounts, meaning dinero disponible for your presupuesto.
lesson08.t020: In Tracking mode, compare efectivo flow against your Projected Savings. If Projected Savings said $550 but efectivo flow shows $200 growth, somewhere $350 went uncategorized or was undercounted. This nudges you to investigate.
lesson08.t023: Net worth is everything you own minus everything you owe - efectivo, investments, property equity, minus debts, mortgages, and loans. It's the single most important number in your financial life, and it should trend upward over time.
lesson08.t024: 📍Net Worth over time — includes all accounts (On Presupuesto and Off Presupuesto). Agrega your mortgage and investment accounts as Off Presupuesto accounts to see the full picture.
lesson08.t026: Gasto Analysis: expense trends over time
lesson08.t027: Gasto Analysis tracks and compares your gastos over a selected period. Usa it to see trends, fluctuations, and areas of high gasto or savings - not just the single biggest categoría you spent on.
lesson08.t028: 📍Gasto Analysis — compare gastos across a selected period to spot trends, fluctuations, and areas of high gasto or savings.
lesson08.t029: How to use this report for better presupuestación
lesson08.t030: After 2–3 months, use this report to ask: which gastos are rising, fluctuating, or showing a savings opportunity? Usa that insight to refine Tracking mode targets or adjust Envelope mode categoría funding.
lesson08.t032: Calendar card: ingresos and gastos by day
lesson08.t033: The Calendar card displays diario ingresos and gastos in a calendar format for a selected period. Usa it to spot payday, rent, subscriptions, or any days that are unusually heavy on the presupuesto.
lesson08.t034: 📍Calendar card — a quick diario view of ingresos and gastos.
lesson08.t036: Custom Reports: when the question is specific
lesson08.t037: Custom Reports let you analyze transacciones as a table or chart: Bar, Line, Area, Donut, or Table. Puedes analyze Payments, Deposits, or Net, split results by categoría, group, beneficiario, cuenta, or mes, and save the report back to the Reports page.
lesson08.t038: 📍Custom report examples — bar, line, area, donut, and table views. Build once, save to the dashboard.
lesson08.t039: Tip: Combine custom reports with tags for powerful specific analysis. Tag all holiday gasto with #holiday2025, then build a custom report filtered to that tag — instant holiday presupuesto summary across all categorías and accounts.
lesson08.t041: Experimental Reports: advanced reports for later
lesson08.t042: These reports are experimental, so they may have bugs, missing functionality, or change later. Do not start here in your first mes; read about them when your data is more mature and you want deeper analysis.
lesson08.t044: A financial-planning report inspired by Your Money or Your Life. It estimates when passive investment ingresos may cover projected gastos using expense categorías, ingresos or investment accounts, a safe withdrawal rate, and an expense projection method.
lesson08.t046: Tracks Presupuesto page numbers over time: Budgeted, Spent, Overspending Adjustment, and cumulative Saldo. It includes presupuesto categorías only, not transfers or Off Presupuesto accounts.
lesson08.t049: Cash Flow graph — budgeted accounts and disponible-dinero movement
lesson08.t050: Net Worth graph — all accounts and net worth
lesson08.t051: Gasto Analysis — expense trends and high-gasto areas
lesson08.t052: Calendar card — diario ingresos and gastos
lesson08.t053: Custom Reports — tables, charts, filters, and saving reports
lesson08.t054: Crossover Point report — experimental financial-planning report
lesson08.t055: Presupuesto Analysis report — experimental presupuesto-number analysis
lesson09.t004: You've completed the core course. This final lesson covers power user shortcuts, answers the most common questions, and gives you a clear framework for choosing the presupuestación workflow that fits you long term.
lesson09.t005: By the end of this lesson you will…
lesson09.t006: Conoce a simple semanal habit to keep your numbers trustworthy
lesson09.t007: Have answers to the most common beginner questions
lesson09.t008: Have a clear framework for choosing between Tracking and Envelope
lesson09.t009: Conoce where to go when you have questions beyond this course
lesson09.t011: Keep Actual Simple: Your Weekly Rhythm
lesson09.t012: The goal is not to build a perfect system. The goal is to keep your numbers trustworthy enough to make better gasto decisions. A simple semanal habit is better than a complex setup you stop using.
lesson09.t015: Import, sincronización, or enter nuevas transacciones.
lesson09.t017: Revisa overspending before it becomes invisible.
lesson09.t018: Check transfers between your own accounts.
lesson09.t022: Creating too many categorías on day one.
lesson09.t023: Using Tracking mode when you actually need efectivo control.
lesson09.t024: Categorizing credit card payments when both accounts are On Presupuesto.
lesson09.t025: Ignoring uncategorized transacciones for too long.
lesson09.t026: Judging reports before you have a full mes of data.
lesson09.t027: Simple regla: if you open Actual once a week and clean up uncategorized transacciones, transfers, and overspending, the presupuesto stays useful without becoming a diario burden.
lesson09.t029: Which presupuestación workflow fits you long term?
lesson09.t030: Do not treat Tracking and Envelope as two steps in one graduation path. Both are valid, but they answer diferente questions: do you want to compare plan vs reality, or control disponible efectivo before gasto?
lesson09.t032: You have a comfortable efectivo buffer and are not worried about running out of efectivo before mes end
lesson09.t033: You want ingresos and expense targets, then a clear plan-vs-reality comparison
lesson09.t034: You prefer lower maintenance and do not want to continually assign every disponible dollar
lesson09.t035: You are comfortable treating red values as target variance, not a efectivo-funding decision
lesson09.t037: Necesitas efectivo control and want to know whether you can afford gasto before it happens
lesson09.t038: You want every dollar to have a clear decision: assigned now or held for next mes
lesson09.t039: You want overspending to force a funding choice or reduce next mes's To Budget
lesson09.t040: You are comfortable maintaining the presupuesto during the mes, not only reviewing it afterward
lesson09.t041: The practical decision: there is no required graduation path from Tracking to Envelope. Elige based on your need for control and the maintenance level you accept. Puedes change modes later, but do not rely on switching as the learning plan because historical presupuesto values do not always carry the same meaning between modes.
lesson09.t044: This is not the full official shortcut list; it is the useful diario set. Open <kbd>?</kbd> inside Actual whenever you need the complete list. On macOS, use <kbd>Cmd+K</kbd> for the Command Palette.
lesson09.t049: Open the Command Palette to search commands and jump to accounts
lesson09.t051: Open the Help Menu with docs, support, and the full shortcut list
lesson09.t053: Toggle the Privacy Filter to hide amounts on screen
lesson09.t055: Undo or redo changes in the current session
lesson09.t057: Import transacciones into the current cuenta
lesson09.t059: Initiate Bank Sync when the cuenta is connected
lesson09.t061: Agrega a new transacción with the date picker open
lesson09.t063: Open Filter when none are selected, or show only selected transacciones
lesson09.t065: While editing: save the value and move down or up
lesson09.t067: While editing: save the value and move right or left
lesson09.t071: Toggle cleared status for selected transacciones
lesson09.t073: Link or view schedule for the selected transacción
lesson09.t075: Merge two selected transacciones when their amounts are equal
lesson09.t076: Full official reference: Keyboard Shortcuts.
lesson09.t077: Some bulk-action shortcuts require one or more transacciones to be selected first. If a shortcut does nothing, select the transacción and try again.
lesson09.t078: macOS note: the docs list Cmd+K for Command Palette on macOS. Other shortcuts may depend on navegador/app behavior, so use ? inside Actual to confirm what is disponible in your setup.
lesson09.t080: Operational topics you will need later
lesson09.t081: These are not part of your diario routine, but they matter once Actual becomes your real financial system. Conoce they exist now, and come back when needed.
lesson09.t083: Before major updates or trying new features, export a backup from Settings. It is not a diario task, but it is an important safety net.
lesson09.t085: The desktop app runs locally without an external servidor, which is fine for getting started. But if you want phone access or multiple devices, a sincronización servidor is the better experience. Sync is not a full replacement for backups.
lesson09.t087: Actual does not currently support native multi-currency. If you have accounts in multiple currencies, read the docs before adding them so you choose the right workaround.
lesson09.t090: Experimental features are optional tools that are still being developed. They may be incomplete, change later, or be removed. Do not make them the foundation of your beginner workflow.
lesson09.t092: Settle accounts, categorías, importing, Rules, and reconciliation before enabling experiments.
lesson09.t094: This matters especially before trying Budget Templates or Rule Action Templating.
lesson09.t095: Enable one feature at a time
lesson09.t096: Entiende its effect on your presupuesto before adding another one.
lesson09.t097: Most useful later: Budget Templates for filling presupuesto amounts, Rule Action Templating for advanced rules, and experimental reports for deeper analysis.
lesson09.t100: P: Do I have to categorize every single transacción?
lesson09.t101: R: Sí - in both modes, every presupuesto-impacting non-transferencia transacción needs a categoría. Off Presupuesto transacciones are not categorized, and transfers between On Presupuesto accounts do not need categorías. Categorías make reports useful and keep the presupuesto working. The good news: Rules handle most categorization automatically after a few weeks.
lesson09.t102: P: In Tracking mode, do unused presupuesto amounts carry over to next mes?
lesson09.t103: R: No. This is a fundamental difference from Envelope mode. In Tracking mode, each mes starts fresh - you set new targets each mes (or copy last mes's with one click). In Envelope mode, leftover dinero in categorías rolls forward automatically and builds up over time.
lesson09.t104: P: Can I sincronización my bank?
lesson09.t105: R: Sí, if your setup and provider are supported. Actual supports SimpleFIN for North America, Pluggy.ai for Brazil, and GoCardless BankAccountData for existing EU/UK users. Bank Sync works with the desktop app through its bundled local servidor, or with an actual-servidor setup, and you click Bank Sync manually to fetch nuevas transacciones. Provider credentials are stored on the local servidor or the servidor you use.
lesson09.t106: P: Is there a aplicación móvil?
lesson09.t107: R: The dedicated mobile apps are no longer maintained. Usa Actual in your phone's navegador - the interface is responsive and works well. On iOS, tap Share → "Agrega to Home Screen" for a full-screen app experience. On Android, use "Agrega to Home Screen" from the navegador menu.
lesson09.t108: P: What if I overspend a categoría badly in Tracking mode?
lesson09.t109: R: Treat it as variance from the target. Puedes leave the red value as a reminder that the plan did not match reality, or increase the target if the original forecast was unrealistic. If you increase the target, Projected Savings decreases, but no efectivo is reserved or moved from another categoría.
lesson09.t110: P: How do I update Actual after a new version is released?
lesson09.t111: R: It depends on how you run Actual. If you use the desktop app, update the app when a new release is disponible. PikaPods auto-updates around a week after each release. Docker users pull the new image. Fly.io has a specific update guide in the official docs at actualbudget.org/docs.
lesson09.t113: You now have everything you need to use Actual Budget confidently. The rest comes from building the semanal habit and letting the data accumulate.

lesson09.t126: Comunidad de Discord — the fastest way to get help from real users
lesson09.t127: GitHub — report bugs and track new features
mode.choice.envelope: Elegiste Envelope Budget. Es el flujo predeterminado y recomendado de Actual para controlar el efectivo disponible. No necesitas cambiar ninguna configuración.
mode.choice.tracking: Elegiste Tracking Budget. Es el flujo tradicional de previsión de ingresos y gastos. Como Actual empieza en Envelope de forma predeterminada, cambia una opción: Settings → Switch to tracking budgeting.
errors.lessonLoadTitle: No se pudo cargar la lección
errors.lessonLoadMessage: Actualiza la página o comprueba que el archivo de la lección exista en assets/lessons/.
errors.screenshotUnavailable: Captura no disponible. Abre la documentación enlazada de Actual si necesitas la imagen más reciente de la interfaz.
